name: Tallinn
summary: Tallinn is like stepping into a real fairy tale! Estonia's capital has a UNESCO-listed Old Town with cobbled streets, towering city walls, and the hilltop Toompea Castle. It's also one of the most digital countries on Earth, where people even vote online.
funFacts: Tallinn's Old Town became a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1997 and still has city walls dotted with around 26 medieval defence towers you can spot.
Skype, the app that let people video-call each other for free, was invented right here in Tallinn back in 2003 by Estonian programmers.
Estonia is so digital that people file taxes, sign documents, and even vote online - kids call it e-Estonia, and it only takes minutes.
Town Hall Square hides Europe's oldest continuously running pharmacy, Raeapteek, open since 1422 - it once sold powdered unicorn horn and burnt hedgehog!
The Estonian language has no future tense and no words for 'he' or 'she' - everyone is just 'tema', and you guess the time from context.
thingsToDo: Walk 15 minutes from the cruise port through Fat Margaret's Tower into the cobbled Old Town and find the warrior weather vane Old Thomas above Town Hall.
Climb Toompea Hill to the pink castle (now Estonia's Parliament) and pop up to Patkuli viewing platform's 157 steps for a postcard view of red rooftops.
Step inside Raeapteek on Town Hall Square, Europe's oldest pharmacy, where a tiny museum shows weird medieval medicines like marzipan, mummy powder, and snake potions.
Explore Lennusadam Seaplane Harbour's giant hangars to climb aboard the real 1936 submarine Lembit, try sub simulators, and follow the cat-shaped family trail.
Wander Telliskivi Creative City, a colourful old factory district full of street art, food trucks, and quirky shops - super photogenic and just outside Old Town.
Hop a quick tram to Kadriorg Park and the Kumu Art Museum, a curvy modern building hiding centuries of Estonian art plus playful kids' workshops.

images.alt: A sweeping view of Tallinn's Old Town with red-tiled rooftops, church spires, and stone walls seen from Toompea hill.
The cobbled Town Hall Square in Tallinn surrounded by colourful old houses and the tall Gothic town hall tower.
The grand Alexander Nevsky Cathedral in Tallinn with its black onion domes topped by golden crosses.
The chunky round medieval tower called Fat Margaret guarding Tallinn's stone city gate.
The pink Kadriorg Palace in Tallinn with bright flower gardens and a fountain in front.
